report_name: Nation-State Actors Weaponize Open-Source ROADtools for Azure Cloud Attacks, Bypassing MFA and Persisting in Networks

threat_actors.name: Midnight Blizzard
threat_actors.description: A nation-state threat actor observed misusing the ROADtools framework to conduct discovery, establish persistence, and evade defenses in Microsoft Azure and Entra ID cloud environments.

threat_actors.name: Curious Serpens
threat_actors.description: A nation-state threat actor observed leveraging the ROADtools framework for discovery, persistence, and defense evasion in cloud environments.

attack_patterns.name: Device Registration
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use the 'roadtx' module of ROADtools to register their own rogue device within a target's Entra ID tenant. This allows the attacker to bypass MFA and Conditional Access Policies, creating a persistent form of access.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The 'roadrecon' module of ROADtools is used to enumerate and dump information from a victim's Entra ID tenant via the Microsoft Graph API. This includes users, groups, service principals, and privileged roles, which are analyzed offline to find attack paths.

attack_patterns.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use ROADtools to manipulate and replay stolen OAuth tokens, such as Primary Refresh Tokens (PRTs), to hijack sessions and maintain access to cloud services as a legitimate user.

tools.name: ROADtools
tools.description: A legitimate open-source framework for Azure and Entra ID security research, co-opted by threat actors to perform discovery, establish persistence via device registration, manipulate OAuth tokens, and evade defenses in cloud environments.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of the Microsoft Azure and Entra ID cloud platforms that are targeted by threat actors using the ROADtools framework.

infrastructure.name: Rogue Registered Device
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled device that is registered within a victim's Entra ID tenant using ROADtools to gain trusted access, bypass MFA, and establish persistence.

indicators.name: ROADtools Default User-Agent
indicators.pattern:
title: ROADtools Default User-Agent
id: 9b1c7f8c-1a2b-4c3d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the default User-Agent string 'roadlib/*' used by the ROADtools framework. While this can be changed by an attacker, its presence is a strong indicator of the tool's usage.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/roadtools-cloud-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.t1098.005
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-useragent|startswith: 'roadlib/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the default User-Agent string 'roadlib/*' used by the ROADtools framework in web proxy or API logs.

indicators.name: Anomalous Entra ID Device Registration
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Entra ID Device Registration
id: 2d3e4f5a-6b7c-4d8e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects device registration events in Microsoft Entra ID, which could indicate malicious use of tools like ROADtools to establish persistence and bypass MFA. Focus on events initiated by non-administrative users or from unusual locations.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/roadtools-cloud-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1098.005
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.azuread
detection:
  selection:
    Operation|contains:
      - 'Add registered owner to device'
      - 'Add registered users to device'
  filter_admin:
    Actor.user.roles:
      - 'Global Administrator'
      - 'Cloud Device Administrator'
  condition: selection and not filter_admin
indicators.description: Detects device registration events in Entra ID audit logs, particularly 'Add registered owner to device' and 'Add registered users to device', which can be indicative of ROADtools abuse.

indicators.name: ROADtools Script Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: ROADtools Script Execution on Endpoint
id: 7c8d9e0f-1a2b-4c3d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the command-line execution of ROADtools scripts 'roadtx.py' and 'roadrecon.py'. This is a high-fidelity indicator of the tool's use on an endpoint.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/roadtools-cloud-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'python roadtx.py'
      - 'python roadrecon.py'
      - 'roadtx.py'
      - 'roadrecon.py'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of ROADtools' main Python scripts (`roadtx.py`, `roadrecon.py`) via command-line logging on endpoints.

indicators.name: Anomalous Azure DRS Token Request
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Azure DRS Token Request
id: 3e4f5a6b-7c8d-4e9f-0a1b-2c3d4e5f6a7b
status: experimental
description: Detects anomalous OAuth token requests for the Azure Device Registration Service (DRS) endpoint. This can indicate an attacker using a tool like ROADtools to register a rogue device.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/roadtools-cloud-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1098.005
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    resourceDisplayName: 'Windows Azure Active Directory'
    resourceId: 'urn:ms-drs:enterpriseregistration.windows.net'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous OAuth token requests for the Azure device registration service endpoint (`urn:ms-drs:enterpriseregistration.windows.net`), a behavior associated with ROADtools device registration.
